## Description / Excerpt

An essay distinguishing moral intent from structural protection, arguing that good faith at scale must be paired with boundaries, consent, and enforceable interfaces.

Excerpt:

There is a quiet confusion at the heart of many of our most painful failures—personal, cultural, and civilizational. It is the confusion between **intent** and **protection**, between **good faith** and **defense**. We are taught, implicitly and explicitly, that if our intentions
